---
title: "IO"
sidebar: api
---

Data loaders for reading sequence data from files and in-memory DataFrames.

## Loading data

| Function | Source |
|---|---|
| `load_csv()` | CSV files |
| `load_json()` | JSON files |
| `load_parquet()` | Parquet files (recommended for large data) |
| `load_dataframe()` | Polars DataFrame (Hive/Spark/Arrow workflows) |
| `load_wide_format()` | Wide-format files (one row per sequence) |

## Saving data

| Function | Format |
|---|---|
| `save_csv()` | CSV |
| `save_json()` | JSON |
| `save_parquet()` | Parquet (with compression) |

## Hive / Spark integration

For data originating from Hive tables or Spark jobs, the recommended workflow is:

1. Export from Spark to Parquet (cloud storage or Unity Catalog Volume)
2. Read with `pl.read_parquet()` or `pl.scan_parquet().collect()`
3. Pass to `load_dataframe()` with a `SequenceConfig` mapping your columns

```python
from yasqat.io import load_dataframe
from yasqat.core.sequence import SequenceConfig

df = pl.read_parquet("s3://bucket/events_export/")
pool = load_dataframe(
    df,
    config=SequenceConfig(
        id_column="user_id",
        time_column="event_ts",
        state_column="event_type",
    ),
    drop_nulls=True,
)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
For small-to-medium tables, use PySpark's `.toArrow()` + `pl.from_arrow()` for a
zero-copy bridge that avoids writing intermediate files.
:::
